mission.title: Install and Configure Langflow
mission.description: Langflow is a powerful platform for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows, providing a visual authoring experience and built-in API servers. It is ideal for developers looking to integrate AI workflows into applications without managing complex dependencies. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes management and potential costs associated with cloud resources.

steps.title: Install the Langflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Langflow CLI tool which is necessary for managing your Langflow application.

```bash

pip install langflow==1.8.0

```

> Ensure Python 3.10-3.13 is installed.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create langflow-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region as necessary.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy Langflow
steps.description:
This command deploys the Langflow application to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langflow-ai/langflow/v1.8.0/deploy/langflow-deployment.yaml

```

> Ensure the deployment YAML is correct for your setup.

steps.title: Post-install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Langflow deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment langflow --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langflow-ai/langflow/v1.8.0/deploy/langflow-monitoring.yaml

```

> Adjust min/max as necessary.
> Ensure monitoring YAML is correct.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Langflow
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Langflow deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langflow-ai/langflow/v1.8.0/deploy/langflow-deployment.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete langflow-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This will delete the entire cluster and all resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Langflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This process includes draining nodes, upgrading the deployment, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl set image deployment/langflow langflow=registry/image:tag

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods

```

> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name.
> Replace with the new image tag.
> Bring the node back online after the upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image cannot be found or accessed.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is crashing due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the application logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'NodeNotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** One or more nodes are not in a ready state, possibly due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check node status with 'kubectl get nodes' and ensure sufficient resources are allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: '503 Service Unavailable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service is not reachable, possibly due to incorrect service configuration.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure the deployment is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved API performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older API endpoints, ensure to update your integrations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.7.0, update your API calls to match the new endpoints.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods' showing all pods in 'Running' status and 'kubectl get services' showing the Langflow service with an external IP.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
